title: Myrtopsis corymbosa

Traditionally used in folk medicine, Corymbanthe has been employed to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Corymbanthe include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Corymbanthe is commonly prepared as a decoction or tea, as well as being used in tincture and infusion forms to leverage its health benefits.
